Source_Species.reason: The paper studies txtH from Streptomyces scabies.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Explicitly stated throughout as Streptomyces scabies.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The strain used is S. scabies 87.22.

Applied_Species.reason: In vivo experiments were performed in Streptomyces scabies.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species is explicitly S. scabies.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: txtH is directly linked to thaxtomin A production in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Thaxtomin A is described as a phytotoxin, and produced by NRPS machinery, so nonribosomal peptide class is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Thaxtomin A was quantified from S. scabies culture extracts; strains were grown as mycelial cultures.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Thaxtomin A is described as the predominant pathogenicity determinant essential for common scab disease development, and virulence/tissue necrosis correlation is discussed.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Deletion of txtH significantly reduced thaxtomin A, reduced virulence on potato tuber slices, and complementation partially restored thaxtomin A production.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TxtH is explicitly identified as an MbtH-like protein (MLP).

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-PCR showed txtH expression under thaxtomin-inducing conditions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same organism/strain; RT-PCR supports expression under thaxtomin-inducing conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used in the paper: deletion, complementation/overexpression with ermEp*, E. coli co-expression, western blot, HPLC, LC-HRESIMS, potato tuber assay, RT-PCR.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Biochemical data show TxtH promotes soluble expression of TxtA-A and TxtB-A, implying interaction with these adenylation domains; the text discusses this interaction explicitly.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summarized findings are directly supported by Results and figures.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Thaxtomin-inducing conditions used OBAC/OBBC with cellobiose/oat bran, and virulence was tested by potato tuber slice assay.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Complementation/overexpression used the strong constitutive ermEp* promoter.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a plausible implication, but the paper does not explicitly propose txtH as an antimicrobial target or discuss application value in those terms.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports reduced txtC transcription likely due to polar effects of the deletion and accumulation of thaxtomin B and D intermediates.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results show S. scabies MLPs can replace txtH when overexpressed, cdaX/clav_p1293 cannot, and the listed conserved residues were tested and found important for solubility-promoting activity.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper identifies SCAB_85461 as the mlp_scab-encoding gene.

Source_Species.reason: mlp_scab is encoded in Streptomyces scabies.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in S. scabies.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: mlp_scab is linked experimentally to thaxtomin A production in double/triple mutant and complementation analyses.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Thaxtomin A is a phytotoxin from an NRPS pathway; this classification is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Thaxtomin A was measured in culture extracts of S. scabies liquid cultures.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Thaxtomin A is described as an essential pathogenicity determinant and correlated with virulence.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Deletion of mlp_scab together with txtH abolished detectable thaxtomin A production, and overexpression of mlp_scab restored production in the triple mutant.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-PCR showed mlp_scab expression under thaxtomin-inducing conditions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same S. scabies system; expression under inducing conditions is shown.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods were used for mlp_scab-related claims except direct single mutant phenotyping was not central; double/triple mutants, overexpression, western blot, HPLC, potato assay, and RT-PCR are all in Results.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MLPscab promoted soluble expression of TxtA-A and TxtB-A in E. coli, supporting functional interaction with these domains.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed finding is directly described in Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Thaxtomin-inducing media and potato tuber slice assay were used.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This application-oriented statement is not made by the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states mlp_scab is native to the scabichelin NRPS cluster, so pathway cross-talk/other metabolite effects are a reasonable direct paper-supported caveat.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states mlp_scab is located in the scabichelin cluster and that in vivo replacement of TxtH required engineered overexpression in the triple mutant.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper identifies SCAB_3331 as mlp_lipo.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were done in S. scabies.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: mlp_lipo is evaluated for effects on thaxtomin A production.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Thaxtomin A is a phytotoxin from an NRPS pathway.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Thaxtomin A was measured in culture extracts from mycelial cultures.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Thaxtomin A is described as an essential pathogenicity determinant and tied to virulence.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Single deletion of mlp_lipo did not alter thaxtomin A production, and overexpression restored thaxtomin A production in the triple mutant.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-PCR showed mlp_lipo expression under thaxtomin-inducing conditions.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Single/double/triple mutant analysis, overexpression with ermEp*, co-expression in E. coli, western blot, HPLC, and RT-PCR are all reported.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MLPlipo promoted soluble expression of TxtA-A and TxtB-A in E. coli, supporting functional interaction with these A-domains.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three claims are directly supported by Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Thaxtomin-inducing cellobiose/oat bran conditions were used.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an inferred application, not an explicit claim from the paper.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper states mlp_lipo is in a putative lipopeptide NRPS cluster, implying perturbation may affect additional metabolites.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says mlp_lipo is in a putative lipopeptide cluster, shares 52.3% identity with TxtH, and can replace TxtH when overexpressed.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: txtA is explicitly within the thaxtomin A biosynthetic pathway/gene cluster.

Enzyme_Name.reason: TxtA is explicitly described as a nonribosomal peptide synthetase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: TxtA contains an adenylation domain; this is stated in Summary.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states TxtA and TxtB A-domains select and activate the appropriate amino acid during thaxtomin biosynthesis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper says appropriate amino acid but does not specify a substrate for TxtA in this article.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: TxtA is part of the NRPS directly involved in thaxtomin biosynthesis.
